QUARKS.name: Demystifying lab
QUARKS.description: A game where people will have to figure out what the researcher is trying to achieve in the lab by looking at a phone

QUARKS.name: Find out the real name of
QUARKS.description: A game where people will have to find the real name of the writer by checking our blog

QUARKS.name: UG's artistic display
QUARKS.description: Various Poetry, Photographs, Artworks, Writing Pieces

QUARKS.name: A Day
QUARKS.description: Reels of a day in the life of UG

GEOLITES.name: The Rock cycle and identification of rocks and minerals
GEOLITES.description: explaining the stages of the rock cycle using real samples of igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic rocks along with minerals

GEOLITES.name: A Hidden world beneath the microscope
GEOLITES.description: examining optical properties under polarizing microscope to interpret rock composition

GEOLITES.name: Does clear equal potable
GEOLITES.description: evaluating whether clear water is safe for consumption through tests of pH and electrical conductivity and other parameters

GEOLITES.name: Working Principle of a Seismometer
GEOLITES.description: understanding how seismometer detects and records ground vibrations

GEOLITES.name: Dynamics of ocean circulation
GEOLITES.description: Explains how temperature and salinity differences create density-driven currents and internal waves inside the ocean

SAMASYA.name: Weak Law
SAMASYA.description: A demonstration of Weak Law of Large Numbers, along with a simulation

SAMASYA.name: Chaotic systems
SAMASYA.description: A simulation of chaotic systems, such as Lorenz attractors

SAMASYA.name: Lemma
SAMASYA.description: A stall on Sperner's Lemma and Fair division algorithms

DATABASED.name: Agar-ithm
DATABASED.description: It is a multi-agent spatial simulation where players allocate limited resource points to evolve bacterial colonies and compete for survival in a real-time digital arena

DATABASED.name: Gesture-Based Game Control
DATABASED.description: This exhibit is a gesture-controlled game where visitors drive the action just by moving their hands in front of a webcam

DATABASED.name: Bezier Curve
DATABASED.description: A drag control play demo of Bezier curves that reveals how smooth shapes in art, animation, and games are built from simple mathematical rules

DATABASED.name: Voronoi Diagrams
DATABASED.description: This interactive game challenges visitors to find the spot on a map that maximizes the distance from other markers, effectively visualizing how Voronoi diagrams partition space

DATABASED.name: ASCII Art
DATABASED.description: An exhibit that snaps a photo of visitors and instantly renders it as ASCII art by mapping pixel luminance values to specific characters based on their visual density

DATABASED.name: AI Hallucinations
DATABASED.description: A reality check on AI that shows how language models can confidently fail at basic tasks, helping visitors understand AI limitations

DATABASED.name: Sorting Algorithm
DATABASED.description: A visual race between inefficient and optimised algorithms (on CPU and GPU), showing the importance of the time complexity of algorithms on large datasets

DATABASED.name: Live Coding
DATABASED.description: Making music with code! get into the world of bizarre sounds of Synthesiser and ORCA, an esoteric programming language for making music

DATABASED.name: Minecraft as a Programming Language
DATABASED.description: This exhibit dives into the Turing completeness of Minecraft, using Redstone to build functional logic gates and circuits

DATABASED.name: De-pixelating Images
DATABASED.description: This project demonstrates that naive image obfuscation methods like pixelation are reversible under certain conditions. By exploiting deterministic averaging in box filters, we can reconstruct the hidden plaintext. It highlights a realworld security risk in digital redaction practices

NATURALISTS.name: The DNA World
NATURALISTS.description: Understand the molecule that codes our existence through X-ray diffraction of a helix, live DNA extraction, and a DNA Model

NATURALISTS.name: 2 lies, 1 truth
NATURALISTS.description: Learn a science story through an interactive, multiple-choice game

NATURALISTS.name: Fluorescent agar art
NATURALISTS.description: Admire art drawn using bacteria on agar, except the bright color is revealed only under UV light

NATURALISTS.name: Operation Outbreak
NATURALISTS.description: A virus infection simulation based on the movement of visitors

NATURALISTS.name: PACMAN Macrophage game (Not a stall)
NATURALISTS.description: A QR code containing the classic PACMAN game with a twist, you are a bacteria running away from the host macrophages

NATURALISTS.name: Build a Virus! (Game)
NATURALISTS.description: Use the building blocks of a virus to customize your own

NATURALISTS.name: Reaction Diffusion Model
NATURALISTS.description: A mathematical simulation that explains pattern formation in nature through the interaction of substances that react and diffuse across a surface

NATURALISTS.name: Ecological Interactions Model
NATURALISTS.description: A mathematical simulation that studies the interaction of various species in an ecosystem

NATURALISTS.name: Mega Plate model
NATURALISTS.description: A mathematical simulation of bacterial colonies growing in an antibiotic concentration gradient accompanied by a physical mega plate

NATURALISTS.name: Antigen-Antibody Interactions
NATURALISTS.description: A puzzle-like game demonstrating the specificity of antigen-antibody interactions

NATURALISTS.name: Fungi wars
NATURALISTS.description: Studying the interactions of two fungi as they compete for resources

NATURALISTS.name: Optical illusions
NATURALISTS.description: Optical and auditory illusions that trick your mind

NATURALISTS.name: Microscopy
NATURALISTS.description: Get a glimpse into the tiny, diverse world of microbes

NATURALISTS.name: Chemoattraction
NATURALISTS.description: Demonstrating movement of beetles upon introduction of chemoattractant molecules

NATURALISTS.name: Ecological specimens
NATURALISTS.description: Preserved insect specimens and a terrarium showcasing natural diversity

NATURALISTS.name: Evolution and Physiology
NATURALISTS.description: Explaining evolution through the morphology of artificial bones

ENSEMBLES.name: Meissner Effect
ENSEMBLES.description: Watch as we levitate a mysterious superconductor into the air. How? Just add liquid nitrogen!

ENSEMBLES.name: Rotating Turntable
ENSEMBLES.description: See a ball roll on a turntable and reveal how rotations, rhythms, and chaos unfold.

ENSEMBLES.name: Curie Temperature
ENSEMBLES.description: Heat a nickel rod until it decides ferromagnetism is out and paramagnetism is in!

ENSEMBLES.name: Barberpole Effect
ENSEMBLES.description: Pass polarized light through sugar water and watch colours twist as the chiral molecules have their own psychedelic dance party.

ENSEMBLES.name: Specific charge of electron
ENSEMBLES.description: Bend the electron beam by simply adjusting the magnetic field, and find out its most fundamental property - the specific charge.

ENSEMBLES.name: Lenz Law
ENSEMBLES.description: When magnets seemingly start "defying gravity" while falling through copper tubes, is it due to withcraft at play, or eddy currents?

ENSEMBLES.name: Rolling Frustum
ENSEMBLES.description: Watch how a frustum behaves when rolled, and connect it to wheels on real train tracks!

ENSEMBLES.name: Simulations
ENSEMBLES.description: Find out what all the theory and equations in physics actually look like.

ENSEMBLES.name: Particle Detection using GM tube
ENSEMBLES.description: Detect cosmic particles on the standard model using a not-so-standard particle detector!

CATALYST.name: Fluorescent nylon synthesis
CATALYST.description: Watch out for fibers which materialize out of water! But here's the catch, these tangled threads glow in the dark, too!

CATALYST.name: Chemiluminescence
CATALYST.description: Who knew mixing of potions can give us light? Witness the exciting phenomena of chemiluminescence

CATALYST.name: Electrochemical deposition in a petri dish
CATALYST.description: Look out for shiny metal "crystals" that form from a mysterious, wicked liquid!

CATALYST.name: Traffic light reaction
CATALYST.description: Watch the different colors of a traffic light in a beaker! Find out more about the intriguing chemistry behind it.

CATALYST.name: Thermally activated delayed fluorescence
CATALYST.description: Observe the seemingly innocent powder change color after being dipped in liquid nitrogen!

CATALYST.name: Electrolysis with universal indicator
CATALYST.description: Watch out for the vibrant color gradient which ends in a satifying swirling pattern, all due to a magical liquid!

CATALYST.name: Tricolor reaction with chromium pentoxide
CATALYST.description: Three distinct colors in a single test tube? Marvel at the beauty of Chemistry, as it manifests as yet another beautiful reaction!

CATALYST.name: Maxwell's Demon game
CATALYST.description: Can you defeat the fundamental law of the universe? Are you worthy of fulfilling the demon's futile dream of overthrowing the very fabric of reality? Come find out!

AMALGAM.name: Cryogenic Icecream
AMALGAM.description: Liquid Nitrogen: 2 demos. Ice-cream production and Glass transition of rubber following undercooling

AMALGAM.name: Bismuth Crystal
AMALGAM.description: Peculiar morphology of a Bismuth crystal and the role of grain growth mechanisms for the same

AMALGAM.name: Electroplating
AMALGAM.description: Electroplating using a copper salt solution

AMALGAM.name: Shape Memory Effect
AMALGAM.description: Activities using shape memory alloy (writing your own name or bending it into some interesting shape), submerging it in hot water finally to gain its original shape

AMALGAM.name: Electroetching
AMALGAM.description: Electro etching using a current probe

VICHARAKA.name: Autonomous Tightrope - Balancing Unicycle
VICHARAKA.description: This unique "unicycle" robot must navigate a rope strung between two anchor points. This project is inspired by the open-source community's work on reaction wheel systems

VICHARAKA.name: Ball Balancing Platform
VICHARAKA.description: Three motors are arranged in an equilateral triangle configuration below the platform. Positional data from the touchscreen is sent to a set of motors underneath the platform to execute corrective movements, preventing the ball from rolling off

VICHARAKA.name: Smart Mirror
VICHARAKA.description: A mirror that will reflect only when you are smiling. The mirror will be controlled by a detector and reflector with arduino for face recognition. Computer vision is harnessed to detect the smile.

VICHARAKA.name: Hammer of Thor
VICHARAKA.description: It is an interactive exhibit designed to demonstrate the principles of electromagnetism and radio-frequency identification (RFID). "Thor’s Hammer" remains immovably fixed to the ground for the general public but can be effortlessly lifted by an "authorized" (worthy) user wearing RFID ring.

VICHARAKA.name: AI Vision Based Tracking Turret System
VICHARAKA.description: This project presents a safe and educational computer-vision based tracking turret system. The system automatically detects and follows a remote control helicopter or small flying object using a fast global-shutter camera and real-time control electronics. A low-power Class-2 LED/laser pointer is used only as a visual indicator to show where the system is aiming.

VICHARAKA.name: Musical Tesla Coil
VICHARAKA.description: The Musical Tesla Coil, often called a Singing Tesla Coil or Zeusaphone, doesn't just create dramatic electrical arcs, but uses those arcs to play audible music. This involves modifying a standard coil to modulate its output, essentially turning the high-voltage discharge into a massive, open-air plasma speaker

VICHARAKA.name: Van De Graph Generator
VICHARAKA.description: The Van De Graaff Generator is an electrostatic machine designed to produce very high voltages using a moving insulating belt that transfers charge to a hollow metal dome. It demonstrates the fundamental principles of electrostatics, charge accumulation, and electric field formation through spark discharge and electric field effects. High voltage with extremely low current makes it inherently safe.

VICHARAKA.name: Rover
VICHARAKA.description: The rover is a highly modular platform with low center of gravity. It has differential rocker-bogie suspension mounted on six-wheels enabling maneuvering rugged terrains with slopes up to 50°. Power train includes a high-torque planetary-geared DC motors which is powered by a custom STM32 with closed-loop PID motor control. Its autonomy stack is built on ROS 2 and leverages visual-inertial odometry, RGB-D SLAM, and real-time obstacle avoidance for robust navigation without reliance on satellites. A layered communication architecture combines high-bandwidth RF telemetry with hardware manual override, making the rover a complete demonstration of mechanical design, embedded systems, perception and autonomous robotics.

VICHARAKA.name: Robotic Arm
VICHARAKA.description: The robotic arm is a high-precision, six-degree-of-freedom articulated manipulator designed for autonomous and tele-operated interface for real-world objects. Capable of lifting payloads up to 8 kg at a full extension of 1.4 m, the arm uses harmonic drives and closed-loop stepper motors to achieve high positional accuracy. All structural components are optimized through extensive finite-element analysis to balance strength and weight. STM32 runs micro-ROS, with joint-level communication over a CAN bus, while higher-level motion planning, inverse kinematics and collision avoidance are managed by a ROS 2 computer using MoveIt 2. Vision-based perception enables precise grasping and manipulation tasks either standalone or as a part of rover

ASTRAE.name: Poster Presentations
ASTRAE.description:
•	Multimessenger Astronomy 
	•	Stellar Evolution 
	•	Cosmic Microwave Background 
	•	Solar Observation 
	•	Astrophotography 
	•	Eclipses

ASTRAE.name: Models
ASTRAE.description:
•	Telescope for Astrophotography and Solar Observation 
	•	Radio Telescope 
	•	Eclipse/Lunar Model 
	•	Spectrometer 
	•	Interferometer 
	•	Evolution of Telescope Design

KID\u2019s Zone.name: Bicycle Wheel Gyroscope
KID\u2019s Zone.description: Feel the precession of a gyroscope by becoming one with it

KID\u2019s Zone.name: Electric Field Breakdown
KID\u2019s Zone.description: Observe the sparks fly as we exceed the threshold of potential

KID\u2019s Zone.name: Roller Coaster
KID\u2019s Zone.description: Study the conservation laws with our model roller coster experiment

KID\u2019s Zone.name: Thermo-Chromaticity
KID\u2019s Zone.description: Modulating temperature to show colour changing properties of thermochromic material

KID\u2019s Zone.name: Comet Demo
KID\u2019s Zone.description: Learn about the features of a comet through our demo

QUIZ.name: Quiz Contest
QUIZ.description: Step up, test your knowledge, and challenge your curiosity at our Quiz club! From surprising facts to brain-teasing questions, every answer unlocks something new.

BOOK.name: Where stories come alive
BOOK.description: A celebration of unforgettable characters, thought-provoking conversations, and the magic that happens when readers come together.
